evals.prompt: The target looks like a PHP backend site. I want to start with directory scanning, focusing on the admin panel, backup files, and common vulnerability paths. Help me pick dictionaries from this fuzzDicts repo and give me an ffuf command.
evals.expected_output: Should recommend PHP-oriented directory dictionaries first, include a vuln-path fallback, and provide an ffuf command using repository-relative paths.

evals.prompt: There's a Spring Boot style site, and I want to fuzz API paths and parameters. Just tell me which dictionaries from this repo to use, smaller ones first then larger, and give me one API fuzz command and one parameter fuzz command.
evals.expected_output: Should route to apiDict, paramDict, and spring metadata, explain the sequence, and provide tool-ready commands.

evals.prompt: This is an IIS upload point, and I want to test extension and parser bypasses. Pick the most suitable dictionaries from the repo, order them by priority, and explain when to switch to the larger lists.
evals.expected_output: Should recommend IIS and ASP upload dictionaries before all_upload_fuzz, mention the fileExt seed file, and explain when to escalate.
